title: Widget setup and integration documentation
description: Document the widget extension in CLAUDE.md (or an inline note): finalize and record the exact App Group identifier (group.net.renalias.AggregatorApp) and Keychain Access Group identifier; explain the requirement to run xcodegen generate after any project.yml change that touches the widget target; describe the widget rotation model (5 timeline entries ~3 min apart, ~30 min data reload, WidgetCenter-triggered reloads); and document the aggregator:// deep-link scheme format for future reference.
acceptance_criteria: App Group and Keychain Access Group identifiers are finalized and written down
CLAUDE.md or equivalent notes the xcodegen requirement after widget-related project.yml changes
Widget refresh model and rotation mechanism are explained
aggregator:// URL scheme and supported path formats are documented

handoff_summary.completed: All four acceptance criteria addressed: identifiers documented (group.net.renalias.AggregatorApp / $(AppIdentifierPrefix)net.renalias.AggregatorApp.shared), xcodegen requirement noted with INFOPLIST_FILE gotcha, refresh model described with concrete numbers from Provider.swift, and aggregator:// URL scheme documented with both supported paths.
handoff_summary.remaining: None — documentation bead is fully complete.
handoff_summary.risks: AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift still wires the stub AggregatorRadarPlaceholderProvider and Color.clear entry view; downstream beads must replace this file entirely. The documented identifiers assume the stub-to-real migration beads have already landed (or will land) cleanly — no documentation change required for that transition.
handoff_summary.next_action: No follow-up needed. The documentation bead is complete.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — CLAUDE.md is not modified by any other in-flight bead. The new section inserts cleanly before the Networking conventions section.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Placed the widget section immediately before Networking conventions (the section it has the most overlap with — Keychain and API auth). Used concrete numbers from Provider.swift (180 s, 1800 s, 5 items) rather than prose approximations, so the documentation stays verifiable against the code.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift still uses the stub provider; the documented refresh behavior describes AggregatorRadarProvider which is in Provider.swift and is the intended live implementation. The bundle will not exercise that code path until a downstream bead wires AggregatorRadarProvider into AggregatorRadarWidget.

conflict_risks: None — CLAUDE.md is not modified by any other in-flight bead. The new section inserts cleanly before the Networking conventions section.

execution_history.summary: Added a 'Widget extension (AggregatorWidget)' section to CLAUDE.md documenting the App Group identifier, Keychain Access Group identifier, xcodegen requirement for project.yml widget changes, the full timeline refresh model (5 entries × 3 min rotation, 30 min reload, offline fallback), widget configuration options, and the aggregator:// deep-link scheme.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Focus only on files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read or modify unrelated documentation.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before writing any documentation, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries to inform documentation; skip entries that don't apply.

Do **not** write to memory — documentation agents are read-only.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not read the full codebase for context. Focus on the changed files and their immediate surroundings.
- Keep documentation updates concise and proportional to the change.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (documentation beads always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of what was documented.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when documentation is complete with no gaps.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the number of unresolved documentation gaps, set `requires_followup=true`, and include `block_reason` when implementation must land first or documentation cannot be completed.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as concise narrative context only; they do not override the structured verdict fields.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.

worker_prompt_context.title: Widget setup and integration documentation
worker_prompt_context.description: Document the widget extension in CLAUDE.md (or an inline note): finalize and record the exact App Group identifier (group.net.renalias.AggregatorApp) and Keychain Access Group identifier; explain the requirement to run xcodegen generate after any project.yml change that touches the widget target; describe the widget rotation model (5 timeline entries ~3 min apart, ~30 min data reload, WidgetCenter-triggered reloads); and document the aggregator:// deep-link scheme format for future reference.
acceptance_criteria: App Group and Keychain Access Group identifiers are finalized and written down
CLAUDE.md or equivalent notes the xcodegen requirement after widget-related project.yml changes
Widget refresh model and rotation mechanism are explained
aggregator:// URL scheme and supported path formats are documented
worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks:
The AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift stub uses @main — the widget implementation bead must replace this file entirely rather than add a second @main. Downstream bead should be aware the stub exists and overwrite it.
Any sibling bead that also modifies project.yml will need to rebase or merge cleanly. The entitlements block is new territory in the file so merge conflicts are low-risk structurally, but all project.yml changers must re-run xcodegen generate after merge.
Any future bead that adds UIKit-importing files to AggregatorApp/Common/ risks breaking the widget target because those paths are shared members of both targets.
No file conflicts expected. APIClient.swift ArticleFeed error is a pre-existing sibling-bead issue that does not overlap this file.
AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift was already modified by sibling bead B-b89c0ea0 (type renames from AggregatorPlaceholder* to AggregatorRadar*). This bead's edit applied cleanly on top of that change. Future beads touching the bundle should be aware both changes are now live on this branch.
AggregatorApp.swift was also modified by bead B-d10d6075 (same commit). Downstream beads that touch AggregatorApp.swift should expect the custom init() and the @State private var credentialsStore: CredentialsStore declaration (no inline default value).
project.yml now uses GENERATE_INFOPLIST_FILE for the widget target (replacing INFOPLIST_FILE). Any downstream bead that re-adds INFOPLIST_FILE to the widget settings will reintroduce the AppIntentsSSUTraining build failure. The AggregatorWidget/Info.plist file still exists on disk but is now ignored by the build system — a future cleanup bead could remove it, but it is harmless as-is.
B-8d75c57e and B-b7a3bc0c both write to Provider.swift. B-8d75c57e must complete before B-b7a3bc0c since the latter implements timeline(for:in:) which uses WidgetImageCache. The dependency chain is already declared correctly.
Any bead that adds GENERATE_INFOPLIST_FILE: YES back to AggregatorApp settings will conflict with the new info key. Downstream beads that modify Info.plist keys must use the info.properties section in project.yml, not INFOPLIST_KEY_ build settings.
B-6c345d37, B-59524c20, B-422672f9 all modify WidgetViews.swift. They need to append to the file rather than overwrite it to preserve RadarDefaultBackground. File has a clear MARK comment delineating the section for layout views.
The stub AggregatorRadarPlaceholderEntry in AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift coexists with the new AggregatorRadarEntry without conflict — both types compile independently. The Provider bead must replace the placeholder.
AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift still declares AggregatorRadarPlaceholderEntry and AggregatorRadarPlaceholderProvider. The views bead that replaces the stub must remove those types to avoid duplicate-symbol conflicts with WidgetEntry and AggregatorRadarProvider.
None anticipated. ThreadsView is the only file owning NavigationStack path state for the Threads tab. AppRoot tab selection is new state not previously owned by any file.
AggregatorRadarEntry gained two new optional fields (publishedAt, importanceScore). Any downstream bead that constructs AggregatorRadarEntry literals will need to provide these (or rely on the default nil since they are Optional).
WidgetViews.swift defines AggregatorRadarEntry (separate type). Any bead that touches WidgetViews or AggregatorWidgetBundle must reconcile entry types with Provider.swift's WidgetEntry.
Provider.swift has uncommitted changes from a sibling bead in the shared worktree. If that bead commits before this branch merges, there is no conflict with this bead's files. The uncommitted Provider.swift state does not affect ArticleReadStore or ArticleListView.
AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift still wires Color.clear as the entry view — downstream beads replacing that placeholder must update to dispatch SmallWidgetView/MediumWidgetView based on WidgetFamily.

last_agent_result.summary: Added a 'Widget extension (AggregatorWidget)' section to CLAUDE.md documenting the App Group identifier, Keychain Access Group identifier, xcodegen requirement for project.yml widget changes, the full timeline refresh model (5 entries × 3 min rotation, 30 min reload, offline fallback), widget configuration options, and the aggregator:// deep-link scheme.
